task:
Implement the following plan:

# Checkpoint Correctness Analysis (Updated Code)

## Context

Analyzing data preservation when the application shuts down shortly after a periodic checkpoint. The shutdown checkpoint covers only a short duration (minutes), not the full retention period. On next startup, the system incorrectly assumes old checkpoint files will remain available indefinitely.

---

## Primary Bug: False assumption about checkpoint coverage after restart

### The Problem

The shutdown checkpoint covers `[lastCheckpoint, shutdown_time]` — potentially just a few minutes. The rest of the retention window depends on older checkpoint files. After restart, the system sets `lastCheckpoint = time.Now()` and marks all loaded data as `archived: true`, effectively "forgetting" that old checkpoint files are the **only** persistent copy of most of the retention window. New periodic checkpoints only cover data from restart onward — they never re-persist the loaded data. When `CleanUp` eventually deletes the old files, data within the retention window is permanently lost from disk.

### Root Cause

**`checkpoint.go:95-96`** — `lastCheckpoint` set to restart time, not to the start of loaded data:
```go
lastCheckpoint = time.Now()  // Should be restoreFrom instead
```

**`checkpoint.go:473`** — Loaded buffers marked as archived, preventing re-checkpointing:
```go
archived: true  // Should be false so data gets re-persisted
```

**`checkpoint.go:280-291`** — `toCheckpointFile` skips all-archived metrics:
```go
if allArchived {
    continue  // Loaded data never re-checkpointed
}
```

### Timeline Showing the Gap

```
Retention = 48h, CheckpointInterval = 12h

T-12h:  Periodic checkpoint → {T-12h}.bin [T-12h, T]
T:      Shutdown checkpoint → {T}.bin [T, T+5min]   ← SHORT
T+1h:   Restart. Loads both files. lastCheckpoint = T+1h.
         All loaded buffers: archived=true

T+13h:  Checkpoint → {T+1h}.bin [T+1h, T+13h]  ← only NEW data
         Old data [T-12h, T+5min]? SKIPPED
